We use cookies

Keepit A/S uses its own cookies and those of third parties to collect data (such as IP address, cookie ID, device information, settings preferences, session identifiers and your activity on the website) about you in order for our website to function properly ("Necessary"), remember your settings and create a better user experience for you ("Functional"), analyze your behavior and to optimize the website ("Statistical") and target our content and ads on social media and external websites based on your behavior ("Marketing "). Information about you may be transferred to our social media, advertising, and analytics partners. The partner may be established in uncertain third countries. It is your decision for which purposes our website may use cookies and thereby also process personal data about you via cookies. By clicking ‘Accept all’ you agree to our general use of cookies. You may at any time withdraw your consent by clicking on the cookie icon at the bottom left of the website. Read more about how we process your data in our Privacy Policy (Updated March 2026). Read more about how we use cookies and other technologies by clicking the link below. Read more about cookies
Strictly necessary cookies help make a website navigable by activating basic functions such as page navigation and access to secure website areas. Without these cookies, the website would not be able to work properly.
Service: Cookie Information
Purpose: Supports the website's technical functions. [Role: Data processor] [Type: Third-party cookie]
Privacy policy: Cookie Information - Privacy policy(opens in a new tab)
Expiry: a year

Service:
Purpose:
Expiry: a year
Name: _zitok
Vendor: .www.keepit.com
powered by: Cookie Information

Cookie policy

Your consent applies to the following domains: keepit.com, lp.keepit.com, help.keepit.com The cookie policy was last updated July 2025

At Keepit, we make an effort to offer a transparent website with the content that is the most relevant for you.

In order to do so, we use cookies and other technologies that can collect, process and sort information regarding how you and other visitors use www.keepit.com ('The Website').

With this policy we wish to give you a complete overview of what the processing of your personal data entails, what you have to be aware of and your options to oppose the processing.

The processing of personal data by the use of cookies

Keepit uses different categories of cookies to provide, protect, improve, and promote our Website and services. Keepit’s use of cookies may include processing of your personal data, and therefore we recommend that you also read our Privacy Policy, which describes our processing of personal data and your rights as a data subject.

Consent

By using the Website and enabling cookies on our cookie preference centre the first time you visit the Website, you consent that Keepit uses cookies as described below. You can change your consent by clicking the relevant button or withdraw your consent by blocking and/or deleting cookies on your web browser.

Your consent applies to the following domains:

  • www.keepit.com
  • lp.keepit.com
  • uk-ld.keepit.com
  • dk-co.keepit.com

What are cookies?

Cookies are small text files containing small amounts of information that are placed on your computer, smartphone or other electronic device when you visit a website. Cookies are widely used by website owners in order to make their websites work, operate more efficiently and to provide reporting information. Cookies help us to remember certain circumstances about your visit to our Website, such as your choices and/or settings. They may also be used to deliver advertisements that are relevant to you.

Cookies can be used by Keepit and third parties. Cookies set by the website owner (in this case, Keepit) are called 'first party cookies'. Cookies set by parties other than the website owner are called 'third party cookies'. Third party cookies enable third party features or functionalities to be provided on or through the website, such as advertising and interactive content. Please refer to our cookie overview for the information if the cookie is a first party cookie or a third party cookie.

Cookies can be divided in two types of cookies, 'session' and 'persistent' cookies. The session cookies are linked to the current visit to our Website and are deleted automatically when you close your web browser. In contrast, persistent cookies will be stored on your device and will delete themselves after a certain period but will be renewed every time you visit the Website. A detailed description may be found in the cookie overview.

This Cookie Policy may change

This Cookie Policy may be subject to periodical changes in the future, due to external reasons (e.g. changes in the relevant law), or internal reasons (e.g. technical modifications to our Website and/or Services or variations in the use of cookies on the Website and/or Services). We reserve the right to amend this Cookie Policy whenever it is required by the circumstances, without prior notice. However, the latest version is always available on our Website.

How long are cookies stored?

The length of time a cookie is stored on your devices and browsers varies. Some cookies are stored on your device only as long as you have your browser open (session cookies) while other cookies are stored on your device for a longer period of time (persistent cookies). The lifetime is calculated according to your last visit to the website. When a cookie expires, it is automatically deleted. All our cookies’ lifetimes are specified in our cookie overview.

How to block or delete cookies

You may at any time block all or just third-party cookies completely by changing the browser settings on your computer, tablet or smartphone. The location of these settings will depend on the browser you use. However, you should be aware that if you block all or just third-party cookies, there may be functions and services that you will be unable to use on the website (because these depend on cookies).

You can opt-out of cookies from Google Analytics here.

How can you delete cookies?

It is easy to delete cookies that you have previously accepted. It depends on which browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, etc.) and device (smartphone, tablet, PC, Mac) you are using. You can typically find this information under settings – Security and Privacy – but this may vary from one browser to another. Specify which device/browser you are using (click the appropriate link):

  • Internet Explorer
  • Microsoft Edge
  • Mozilla Firefox
  • Google Chrome
  • Opera
  • Safari
  • Flash cookies
  • Apple
  • Android
  • Chrome, Android

Changing your consent

You can change your consent by either deleting cookies from your browser or by changing your original choice. Remember: If you use more than one browser, you must delete cookies in all of them.

Do you have any questions?

If you have any comments or questions relating to the information in this cookie policy and/or processing of personal data, please feel welcome to contact us at dpo@keepit.com . The cookie policy can be updated on the ad hoc basis by Keepit, apart from that it is updated once a month by Cookie Information. If you have any questions about the cookie policy, you are welcome also to contact Cookie Information on their website.

powered by: Cookie Information

  • Statistical
